mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer demand that immediately boosts corporate earnings expectations and investor sentiment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: If the rank correlation between weekly changes in RSAFS and subsequent 5-day SPX returns is less than 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and economic output, which supports higher corporate earnings over the medium term and drives positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the rank correlation between trailing 3-month INDPRO growth and forward 60-day SPX returns is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) over a 5-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls to very low levels (≤3.5%), the labor market becomes extremely tight, creating wage inflation pressures that erode corporate margins and force the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, resulting in negative stock market returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE falls to or below 3.5%, the forward 20-day SPX return is positive in more than 50% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above 2.5%, monetary policy moves into restrictive territory, increasing borrowing costs and reducing the present value of future corporate cash flows, which depresses equity prices over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If after FEDFUNDS crosses above 2.5%, the average forward 20-day SPX return is not negative (one-sided test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in the federal funds rate and decline in the unemployment rate signals policy tightening into an already hot labor market, a combination that historically prefigures equity market corrections as the economy risks overheating.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increasing over 3 months AND UNRATE decreasing over 3 months
failure_condition: If in periods where both conditions hold, forward 20-day SPX returns are not negative on average (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) are rising while industrial production (INDPRO) is falling, it indicates a decoupling where consumer demand outpaces supply, leading to unwanted inventory accumulation and future production cuts that signal an impending economic slowdown and bearish equity market.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth positive AND INDPRO 3-month growth negative
failure_condition: If in such divergence periods, forward 60-day SPX returns are not negative (t-test p > 0.10) or if the joint signal does not produce negative returns in at least 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle regime characterized by low unemployment and decelerating payroll growth, further gains in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) paradoxically signal peaking economic momentum, as the labor market tightens beyond sustainable levels, leading to negative equity returns.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion (UNRATE below 4% and PAYEMS growth slowing)
failure_condition: If in the late-cycle regime, positive payroll growth is followed by positive SPX returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle (defined as FEDFUNDS having increased by at least 50bp over the prior 6 months), an unexpected rise in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) forces the Federal Reserve to raise rates more aggressively than anticipated, which compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and lower growth expectations.
failure_condition: If in tightening cycles, monthly CPI inflation surprises are not followed by negative forward 20-day returns (test of proportions p > 0.10).
